frameworks.name: Kalman-filter ideal observer
frameworks.one_line: Optimal recursive integration of current input with prior estimate.

frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: Perceptual judgments biased toward recently viewed stimuli.

core_question: Do judgments of female body size show serial dependence consistent with optimal Bayesian-like integration of recent visual history?
key_assumptions: Body size is a perceptual magnitude judgeable on a continuous scale
An ideal observer integrates current input with a prior derived from recent stimuli
Bodyline (analogous to numberline) yields reliable magnitude estimates
manipulations: Sequentially presented female body images of varying size
Position along a bodyline anchored by extreme sizes
Trial-to-trial sequence relationships between prior and current body
key_findings: Participants performed the bodyline task with low variability (<6% of scale)
Size judgments were systematically biased toward the previously viewed body
Magnitude of serial dependence was well predicted by a simple Kalman-filter ideal-observer model
interpretations: Serial dependence in body size judgment reflects an optimal, adaptive integration that improves performance
Past visual experiences contribute mechanistically to perception of body size
